free_text: Excitation functions of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on natural tungsten up to 50 MeV
Natural W foils 24.5 and 44.8 microns from Goodfellow
used. Stacks were irradiated with deuteron beams of
24.5 MeV at VUB and 44.8 MeV at LVN cyclotrons. Beam
current was measured in Faraday cup and was monitored
by reactions on Al. Aluminum, zirconium and molybdenum
foils were inserted into the stacks for simultaneous
investigation. The energy of the incident beam was
determined by magnetic deflection at LVN and by TOF at
VUB. Gamma spectrometry was used without chemical
separation. Foils thickness was determined by measuring
the weight and area of the samples. Nuclear data were
Experimental data were assigned to the mid energy point
Ziegler's stopping formula was used to
estimate the energy and its uncertainty.
Total errors were given taking the square root
of the of the sum of the square of the individual
The uncertainties of time information are not
included. They are non-linear and negligible.
